MOTION / Notice Of Motion & Motion For Reconsideration
Off Calendar Sep-14-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. DANIEL BASS, Esq. of Kick Law Firm (Tel: 417-766-4597) appearing for plaintiff;
Atty. JAIME LAURENT, Esq. of Littler Mendelson (Tel: 310-772-7225) for Defendants UPS Supply Chain Solutions, Inc. and UPS.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT UPS SUPPLY CHAIN SOLUTIONS, INC, UNITED PARCEL SERVICE, INC. MOTION / Notice Of Motion & Motion For Reconsideration.

Defense counsel withdraws intent to contest. Matter not argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling: Off calendar. On August 3, 2020, defendants appealed the court's order denying the motion to compel arbitration. The court therefore lacks jurisdiction to reconsider that ruling. (See CCP 916(a); Varian Medical Systems, Inc. v. Delfino (2005) 35 Cal.4th 180, 190 ["an appeal from the denial of a motion to compel arbitration automatically stays all further trial court proceedings on the merits."].) In any event, the court notes there is a substantial body of authority holding that delivery workers who transport packages through to the conclusion of their journeys in interstate and foreign commerce are exempt from the FAA as transportation workers engaged in interstate commerce, even if the drivers transport goods wholly within the boundaries of a single state. (Rittmann v. Amazon.com, Inc. (9th Cir. Aug. 19, 2020) --- F.3d ----, 2020 WL 4814142; Waithaka v. Amazon.com, Inc. (1st Cir. 2020) 966 F.3d 10, 13 ["last-mile delivery workers are transportation workers 'engaged in . . . interstate commerce,' regardless of whether the workers themselves physically cross state lines"]; see Harden v. Roadway Package Sys., Inc. (9th Cir. 2001) 249 F.3d 1137, 1140 [holding that delivery driver for predecessor of FedEx fell within the section 1 exemption even though there was no indication the driver himself actually crossed state lines].)

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com. (302/EPS)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-17-2020 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Daniel Bass, Esq. of THE KICK LAW FIRM APC, T:310.395.2988, for plaintiff/opposing party;
No other appearance.

RULING: There being no objection, the tentative ruling is adopted as follows:

Defendants UPS Supply Chain Solutions, Inc. and United Parcel Service, Inc.'s demurrer is ordered off calendar for failure to comply with CCP 430.41. "Before filing a demurrer?the moving party shall meet and confer?with the party who filed the pleading that is subject to demurrer for the purpose of determining whether an agreement can be reached that would resolve the objections to be raised in the demurrer." (Id.) Plaintiffs' counsel declares: "During our meet and confer attempts Defendants' counsel never discussed a demurrer or the basis for such a demurrer, but rather threatened a motion for sanctions pursuant to CCP 128.7 if Plaintiffs did not withdraw their complaint." Defendants' counsel's declaration is not to the contrary; indeed, the word "demurrer" appears only in its penultimate sentence, where "filing of the instant Demurrer" is noted. The parties are ordered to meet and confer immediately in compliance with CCP 430.41 and defendants have until February 14, 2020 to respond to the complaint.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com
